title: Thalictrella ludlowii
taxonomy: Thalictrella ludlowii is a genus of flowering plants in the family Brassicaceae. It is a small genus of succulent plants that are native to the Himalayas. The exact classification of Thalictrella ludlowii is within the order Brassicales. Thalictrella ludlowii is closely related to other genera within the Brassicaceae family. Further research is needed to confirm its exact taxonomic classification.

intro: Thalictrella ludlowii, also known as Himalayan meadow rue, is a flowering plant species native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Thalictrella ludlowii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being used in Ayurvedic formulations like chyawanprash and triphala.
